title: Glasgow Prestwick showcases cool chain capability to India

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Senior diplomatic visit highlights growing UK-India trade opportunities ahead of free trade agreement implementation.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: photo_camera L-R: Kevin Braidwood, deputy chief executive, South Ayrshire Council; Jules Matteoni, operations director, Glasgow Prestwick Airport; Nico Le Roux, cargo business development director, Glasgow Prestwick Airport; Siddharth Malik, Consul General, and officials from the High Commission of India. Glasgow Prestwick Airport (PIK) has underlined its ambitions to capture a greater share of UK-India trade flows after hosting the Consul General of India, Siddharth Malik, to showcase its upgraded cool chain capabilities.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: The Consul General toured the airport’s cargo facilities alongside Kevin Braidwood, deputy chief executive of South Ayrshire Council, and the local authority’s business development officer, Calum McPhail. Talks centred on the forthcoming UK-India Free Trade Agreement, expected to be implemented in April, and the additional bilateral trade volumes it could unlock.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Glasgow Prestwick showcases cool chain capability to India
